# Zonted

> Honest writing on AI in production by Bernard Huang. Operator notes from someone building fully autonomous AI businesses — agent workflows, model selection, cost economics, and what actually breaks. Receipts included.

Zonted is Bernard Huang's personal site. The angle is non-commodity: specific systems, real numbers from production, counterintuitive takes earned from running AI pipelines daily. Bernard runs Tabiji (an AI-driven travel publisher) and writes about the systems behind it — token herding agent swarms, cron-shipped content pipelines, API economics, model benchmarks. He founded Clearscope (the bootstrapped SEO content optimization platform) and has been in SEO/content for 15+ years. He treats AI the same way he treats search: empirical, measured, skeptical of received wisdom.

## What this site covers

- **Token herding** — orchestrating large numbers of AI calls across models, weighing cost vs. quality per task
- **AI video, image, and music generation** — first-hand benchmarks of every commercial model worth running
- **Content pipelines** — Reels, comics, articles, video produced end-to-end by agent swarms
- **Search and answer-engine optimization (AEO)** — what works for AI-driven search after Google Zero
- **API economics** — cost-per-call analysis, infrastructure tradeoffs, the dying scraping economy
- **Operator philosophy** — why "slop, iterate, curate" beats hand-crafting; why owning the stack matters

## All posts, with outlines (50)

### How to Turn a Photo Into Game-Ready Pixel Sprites for $5
https://zonted.com/posts/pixelforge-launch/  ·  2026-06-10
PixelForge is live. Upload a portrait, get a game-ready pixel sprite pack — 4×4 walk sheet, transparent frames, engine files — for $5. The pipeline, plus the first $5.
Sections: What five dollars buys · How it works: generated by AI, finished by code · The build: days, not weeks · Ship or Die · The first $5

### What Is Vibe Trading? I Let 3 AIs Trade Real Money
https://zonted.com/posts/vibe-trading/  ·  2026-06-10
I let three AIs research, backtest, and trade real money through Robinhood's new agentic account. ~310 strategies later, buy-and-hold is still undefeated.
Sections: What vibe trading is · The $100 book · “Backtests become fan fiction” · The edge that lived in the data feed · Surprise: there's a live $16k book · 250 strategies and the lottery math · The boring ending (which is the point) · What ~310 strategies taught me · Try this at home (the starter kit)

### PalmAura Post-Mortem: Apple Rejected My Vibe-Coded App Twice Under Guideline 4.3(b)
https://zonted.com/posts/palmaura-postmortem/  ·  2026-06-09
Five builds, two rejections, same guideline both times. What Apple's saturated-category spam wall means for vibe-coded apps, why agentic iOS development feels slow next to the web, and the lesson I never got to take.
Sections: What I shipped · Two rejections, one guideline · The loop is the product · The class I never got to take · To the graveyard

### Does Threatening an AI Actually Get You Better Work? I Tested It 12 Times
https://zonted.com/posts/stakes-priming-surface-area/  ·  2026-06-04
I re-ran my threaten-the-AI experiment properly: 3 tasks, 4 levels of stakes, 12 blind-graded sessions. The stakes dial doesn't exist; discretionary surface area does.
Sections: What the first post showed — and didn't · The design · Twelve runs, one table · What survived · Surface area, not stakes · The caveats, because this is the whole point

### The May Recap: Miami, a Google Wipeout, and $600 in the Red
https://zonted.com/posts/may-2026-recap/  ·  2026-06-01
May 2026 recap: Consensus in Miami and the plan-three-times build technique, Google wiping Tabiji's search traffic overnight, ~$600/month in the red, and the four lessons that compounded.
Sections: Consensus, Miami · Google wiped Tabiji’s search traffic · The numbers · What May taught me · Next

### How My Agent Produced a 3.9M-View Viral Video
https://zonted.com/posts/how-my-agent-made-a-viral-video/  ·  2026-05-31
A step-by-step teardown of one autonomous AI video: the Tulum spare-tire scam reel that hit 3.9 million views. The real queue JSON, the exact Seedance prompt, the FFmpeg overlay code, and the publish flow.
Sections: The video · The pipeline, in one line · Step 1: the concept is data, not a script · Step 2: concept → documentary prompt · Step 3: generate the clip · Step 4: burn the overlay · Step 5: publish everywhere at once · Why this one hit 3.9 million · The takeaway

### The Three Unlocks Behind 18M Views of AI Travel Content
https://zonted.com/posts/tabiji-18m-views/  ·  2026-05-31
Tabiji, our AI travel-safety channel, got 18,117,121 views in 90 days — 98.6% from strangers. The three unlocks: Reddit as a demand oracle, warming accounts to read as human, and one hired human doing the engagement we refused to automate.
Sections: The receipt · Unlock 1: Reddit is a demand oracle · Unlock 2: a new account is guilty until proven human · Unlock 3: we hired a human to do the un-automatable part · The machine · What 18 million views is actually worth · The takeaway

### Kapiko: a 10-day, $150 post-mortem
https://zonted.com/posts/kapiko-postmortem/  ·  2026-05-30
Built and shipped Kapiko — an AI-generated ambient music YouTube channel — in 10 days for ~$150. The pipeline worked. The Suno API does not exist. Here is the autopsy and what is actually reusable.
Sections: How Kapiko started · How the system worked · How Kapiko scored as a fully AI autonomous business · The genre is real · Cause of death · What’s reusable · Next

### I Made GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok Take the Attachment Test: They All Came Back Secure
https://zonted.com/posts/ai-attachment-secure/  ·  2026-05-26
Fifth post in the personality-testing series. Same four models, attachment theory (ECR-R) this time. Every model came back Secure, but the spread inside the quadrant tells a different story about how each one relates to you.
Sections: How attachment theory works · What each model came back as · The data · Why every AI is securely attached · The methodology spread · Tune your agent to your attachment style · Wrapping up

### I Made GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok Take the DISC Test: They All Came Back C-Dominant
https://zonted.com/posts/ai-disc-c-dominant/  ·  2026-05-26
Fourth post in the personality-testing series. Same four models, fourth instrument, and we're back to convergence: every AI lands C-dominant CS-blend on DISC, even Grok. Here's why the instrument's resolution determines what you can see.
Sections: What each model came back as · The data · Why DISC pulls us back to convergence · The methodology spread · Tune your agent to your DISC type · Wrapping up

### I Made GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok Take the Enneagram Test: Each One Was a Different Type
https://zonted.com/posts/ai-enneagram-different-types/  ·  2026-05-26
The MBTI said all AIs are INTJ. The Big Five said three of four are the same. The Enneagram says each one is a different type. Same models, sharper instrument, very different story.
Sections: What each model came back as · A controlled experiment: same model, different harness, flipped profile · Three tests, three different answers · Tune your agent to your Enneagram type · Wrapping up

### The Devil Is in the AI Skills
https://zonted.com/posts/devil-is-in-the-ai-skills/  ·  2026-05-25
Stock AI couldn't draw my fiancée. An open-source skill on GitHub could. The model is a commodity; the skill is the moat.
Sections: Round one: stock AI · Why stock AI couldn't draw · Round two: a skill from GitHub · The receipts · Then I built a game · The devil is in the AI skills · Chauffeur knowledge · The takeaway

### I Made GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, GLM, MiniMax Take the MBTI Test: They All Came Back INTJ
https://zonted.com/posts/every-ai-is-intj/  ·  2026-05-25
Opus, GPT-5.5, Gemini, GLM, Grok, MiniMax — 100 OEJTS administrations each, 597 of 600 came back INTJ. Every frontier AI thinks it's the same person. Here's why that matters.
Sections: How I tested this · The results · Why this happens · So what · Tune the agent yourself · Caveats · Wrapping up

### I Made GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok Take the Big Five Test: 3 of 4 Came Back the Same Person
https://zonted.com/posts/three-of-four-ais-same-person/  ·  2026-05-25
The MBTI finding held up on the real test. Claude, GPT, and Gemini all scored identical Big Five personalities. Grok was the only one that came back different — and the difference is exactly what xAI markets.
Sections: Three of four are the same person · Then there’s Grok · A quick aside on methodology · So what does this mean for you · Wrapping up

## Sections

- [All posts](https://zonted.com/posts/): Complete archive — 50 essays, updated regularly
- [Games](https://zonted.com/games/): Browser games built in the lab, including playable project post-mortems
- [AI Stack](https://zonted.com/ai-stack/): The tools and models actually running in production right now
- [Portfolio](https://zonted.com/portfolio/): Companies built or in-build with AI agents
- [Metrics](https://zonted.com/metrics/): Live operator dashboard — traffic, books shipped, revenue
- [About](https://zonted.com/about/): Background, current focus, how to get in touch

## Feeds

- [RSS](https://zonted.com/feed.xml) — full archive with content excerpts
- [Email newsletter](https://zonted.com/#subscribe) — one email when a new post ships, no drip sequences

## Author

Bernard Huang. Based in Austin, TX. Founder of [Clearscope](https://clearscope.io) — the bootstrapped SEO content optimization platform he built and grew profitably for years before exiting day-to-day operations. Background in SEO/content/affiliate going back ~15 years. Currently building Tabiji, VeracityAPI, AgentTune, PixelForge, and a handful of other agentic businesses. Writes at zonted.com daily-ish.
